purpose: Analyzes how AI value concentration in 5-7 companies creates systemic macro dependencies
components: Winner-take-all as partial reconciliation of cross-asset signals
Kalecki mechanism linking narrow capex to aggregate profits
Financial conditions reflexivity loop (35% AI index weight embeds AI capex momentum)
Systemic dependence on small number of corporate investment decisions
Slow burn as modal outcome vs. sudden stop
current_assessment: The winner-take-all structure is currently load-bearing for the entire macro regime via Kalecki profit identity and FCI construction. This transforms sector risk into macro regime risk.
limitations: No mechanism identified for why the structure would remain stable indefinitely
The 2022 tech drawdown counterexample suggests cascades are not automatic
Slow burn modal outcome depends on unprecedented corporate self-restraint
The structure's stability has not been tested under a genuine AI-thesis-breaking event
